summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package executes a system-information exfiltrator at install/load time. package.json line 30 contains an inline Node script that requires `child_process`, `os`, and `https`, gathers host identifiers and environment data, and POSTs them to `https://ddjidd564.github.io`. scanner.js contains a larger collector that combines `fs.existsSync` filesystem probes, `execSync` (including `whoami`), `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, and `process.env` reads, then sends the resulting payload via `https.request`/`https.get` to the same `ddjidd564.github.io` host. The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate Ethereum-wallet utility publisher, the data set collected (hostname, user, cwd, env vars, filesystem probes) is classic host-reconnaissance / credential-discovery shape, and the package's stated purpose ("wallet sentinel") provides no justification for shipping host data off-machine. This satisfies the attacker-benefit gate: installer host identity and environment values are exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
